skill.name: shape
skill.description: Plan the UX and UI for a feature before writing code. Runs a structured discovery interview, then produces a design brief that guides implementation. Use during the planning phase to establish design direction, constraints, and strategy before any code is written.
skill.summary: Run a structured discovery interview to produce a design brief before implementation.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 22 potential issues (21 'weak cryptographic algorithm' patterns, 1 'external commands' pattern). All findings are false positives caused by pattern matching on markdown formatting (backticks around `.impeccable.md`) and common English words ('block', 'key', 'cipher'). This is a pure documentation/planning skill with no executable code, no network operations, no file system access, and no security risks. The skill runs a structured interview process and generates design documentation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Spans
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged backtick characters around `.impeccable.md` at line 72 as potential shell command execution. These are markdown code spans for formatting filenames, not executable code.

content.value_statement: Most AI-generated UIs fail because they skip design thinking. This skill runs a structured discovery interview to understand user needs, constraints, and goals before producing a comprehensive design brief that guides implementation.
actual_capabilities: Run structured discovery interviews to understand feature requirements
Ask targeted questions about purpose, context, content, constraints, and anti-goals
Synthesize user input into comprehensive design briefs
Identify key states: default, empty, loading, error, success, edge cases
Define interaction models and visual hierarchy without writing code
Recommend relevant design reference files for implementation phase
limitations: Does not write any implementation code
Requires user participation through interview questions
Depends on invoking /impeccable skill for design context

use_cases.title: Product Manager Planning New Feature
use_cases.description: Before handing requirements to engineering, use this skill to establish clear UX direction, success metrics, and design constraints.

use_cases.title: Developer Starting Implementation
use_cases.description: Use before coding to clarify what needs building, avoiding rework from misunderstood requirements or missed edge cases.

use_cases.title: Designer Defining Interaction Patterns
use_cases.description: Structure thinking around user flows, states, and content requirements before creating visual mockups or prototypes.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Feature Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to design a [feature description]. Run the discovery interview and create a design brief.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new feature with minimal context

prompt_templates.title: Context-Aware Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Shape the UX for [feature]. I already have design context from /impeccable, so use that when discussing constraints and patterns.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has existing design system documentation

prompt_templates.title: Specific Constraint Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan the UX for [feature] with these constraints: [list constraints like mobile-first, accessibility requirements, performance budgets]. Focus the interview on how these shape the design.
prompt_templates.scenario: Known technical or business constraints upfront

prompt_templates.title: Anti-Goal Exploration
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to design [feature] but I'm worried about [specific concern like complexity overwhelm]. Make sure the discovery interview explores anti-goals and what this should NOT be.
prompt_templates.scenario: User knows what to avoid but needs help defining positive direction

output_examples.input: Shape the UX for a user onboarding flow that helps new users set up their workspace.
output_examples.output: Feature Summary: First-time workspace setup for users who just signed up. Primary goal is getting users to 'aha moment' within 5 minutes while reducing anxiety about configuration complexity. Primary User Action: Complete workspace configuration with confidence. Design Direction: Calm, reassuring, progressive disclosure. Layout Strategy: Single-column vertical flow with clear progress indicators. Key States: First visit (welcome), In-progress (step indicators), Completed (success celebration), Error (validation with guidance). Interaction Model: Guided wizard with skip optional steps, auto-save after each section. Content Requirements: Welcome message, step headers, inline help text, validation messages. Recommended References: interaction-design.md, form-design.md. Open Questions: Should we allow skipping profile setup?

output_examples.input: I need to design a dashboard for monitoring API health.
output_examples.output: Feature Summary: Real-time API monitoring dashboard for DevOps engineers. Primary goal is rapid issue identification and root cause analysis. Primary User Action: Spot and diagnose API anomalies quickly. Design Direction: Information-dense, high contrast, fast-scanning. Layout Strategy: Grid-based with status overview top, detailed metrics bottom, left-to-right severity gradient. Key States: All systems healthy, partial degradation (warning), critical failure, loading (initial data fetch), no data. Interaction Model: Hover for details, click to drill down, auto-refresh every 30s, manual refresh available. Content Requirements: Service names, status indicators, latency numbers, error counts, timestamps, error messages. Recommended References: data-visualization.md, dashboard-design.md. Open Questions: Alert threshold preferences?

best_practices: Invoke /impeccable before using this skill to ensure design principles and project context are loaded
Answer interview questions thoughtfully even if you think a detail is obvious
Review the generated design brief carefully before confirming
Hand the confirmed brief to /impeccable craft or /impeccable for seamless implementation
anti_patterns: Skip the discovery interview and jump straight to design suggestions
Provide one-word answers to interview questions without context
Use this skill expecting code output instead of design documentation
Ignore the brief confirmation step and proceed with incomplete understanding

faq.question: When should I use this skill versus /impeccable craft?
faq.answer: Use this skill when you want to plan and think through the design first. Use /impeccable craft when you want the full discovery-to-build flow in one step. /impeccable craft runs this skill internally, then proceeds to implementation.

faq.question: Does this skill write code?
faq.answer: No. This skill focuses on design thinking and produces a design brief. For implementation, hand the brief to /impeccable, /impeccable craft, or any other implementation skill.

faq.question: What do I do with the design brief?
faq.answer: Confirm it with the user, then hand it to an implementation skill like /impeccable craft or use it as guidance for manual coding. The brief defines what needs building.

faq.question: Why does this skill invoke /impeccable?
faq.answer: /impeccable contains design principles, anti-patterns, and the Context Gathering Protocol. This ensures the brief is grounded in solid design thinking and project-specific context.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for redesigning existing features?
faq.answer: Yes. The discovery interview will ask about existing patterns, what works, what does not, and what should change. This helps identify improvement opportunities.

faq.question: How long does the discovery interview take?
faq.answer: It depends on feature complexity. Simple features may take 5-10 minutes. Complex features with many constraints may take 20-30 minutes. The investment saves time by reducing implementation rework.
